remarks.title: Registration Comments
description: To report suspected security issues specific to
traffic emanating from Microsoft online services,
including the distribution of malicious content
or other illicit or illegal material through a
Microsoft online service, please submit reports
to:
* https://cert.microsoft.com.
For SPAM and other abuse issues, such as Microsoft
Accounts, please contact:
* abuse@microsoft.com.
To report security vulnerabilities in Microsoft
products and services, please contact:
* secure@microsoft.com.
For legal and law enforcement-related requests,
please contact:
* msndcc@microsoft.com
For routing, peering or DNS issues, please
contact:
* IOC@microsoft.com

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects value modification of registry key containing path to binary used as screensaver.
Detects the creation of screensaver files (.scr) outside of system folders. Attackers may execute an application as an ".SCR" file using "rundll32.exe desk.cpl,InstallScreenSaver" for example.
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split), wagga (name)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by user inactivity.
Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious File Access to Browser Credential Storage
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects file access to browser credential storage paths by non-browser processes, which may indicate credential access attempts.
Adversaries may attempt to access browser credential storage to extract sensitive information such as usernames and passwords or cookies.
This behavior is often commonly observed in credential stealing malware.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: frack113, X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems), Parth-FourCore

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: rundll32 run dll from internet
Detects malicious URL shortcut execution

Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like "C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie".
This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on a WebDav server).
Detects value modification of registry key containing path to binary used as screensaver.
Detects the creation of screensaver files (.scr) outside of system folders. Attackers may execute an application as an ".SCR" file using "rundll32.exe desk.cpl,InstallScreenSaver" for example.
Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.

Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension
Code integrity failures may indicate tampered executables.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: LOLBAS rundll32 with unexpected forward slash paths (via cmdline)
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Rundll32 is often passed a path to a DLL. Typically these paths utlize backslashes (e.g. C:\windows\system32). Some malware campaigns have been observed using forward slashes (e.g. C:/windows/system32).

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Creation of an Executable by an Executable
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of an executable by another executable

signature_info.copyright: Copyright 2012 Google Inc. All rights reserved.

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET MALWARE Miniduke variant C&C activity
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET MALWARE Miniduke variant C&C activity"; flow:to_server,established; http.uri; content:"&Auth="; content:"&Session="; distance:0; content:"&DataID="; distance:0; content:"&FamilyID="; distance:0; reference:md5,8bbc55ec1a7e86cb21d3cda5ccb43e1e; reference:url,www.f-secure.com/static/doc/labs_global/Whitepapers/cosmicduke_whitepaper.pdf; classtype:targeted-activity; sid:2023909; rev:5; metadata:created_at 2014_07_03, former_category MALWARE, updated_at 2020_05_21;)
alert ( gid:122; sid:1; rev:2; msg:"(port_scan) TCP portscan"; metadata: rule-type preproc; classtype:attempted-recon;)
alert ( gid:119; sid:228; rev:2; msg:"(http_inspect) server response before client request"; metadata: rule-type preproc; classtype:unknown;)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: POLICY-OTHER HTTP request by IPv4 address attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tcp any any -> any $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"POLICY-OTHER HTTP request by IPv4 address attempt"; flow:to_server,established; http_header; content:"Host:",fast_pattern,nocase; pcre:"/^Host\x3a\s*(?:(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9]?[0-9])\.){3}(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9]?[0-9])\s*:?\s*\d*\s*$/smi"; service:http; reference:url,www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec15.html; classtype:policy-violation; sid:50447; rev:1; )

sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, frack113 (split), wagga (name)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by user inactivity.
Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension

attributes.magic: PE32 executable (GUI) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of screensaver files (.scr) outside of system folders. Attackers may execute an application as an ".SCR" file using "rundll32.exe desk.cpl,InstallScreenSaver" for example.

Code integrity failures may indicate tampered executables.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: LOLBAS rundll32 with unexpected forward slash paths (via cmdline)
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Rundll32 is often passed a path to a DLL. Typically these paths utlize backslashes (e.g. C:\windows\system32). Some malware campaigns have been observed using forward slashes (e.g. C:/windows/system32).

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Creation of an Executable by an Executable
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the creation of an executable by another executable

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET MALWARE Miniduke variant C&C activity
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET MALWARE Miniduke variant C&C activity"; flow:to_server,established; http.uri; content:"&Auth="; content:"&Session="; distance:0; content:"&DataID="; distance:0; content:"&FamilyID="; distance:0; reference:md5,8bbc55ec1a7e86cb21d3cda5ccb43e1e; reference:url,www.f-secure.com/static/doc/labs_global/Whitepapers/cosmicduke_whitepaper.pdf; classtype:targeted-activity; sid:2023909; rev:5; metadata:created_at 2014_07_03, former_category MALWARE, updated_at 2020_05_21;)
alert ( gid:122; sid:1; rev:2; msg:"(port_scan) TCP portscan"; metadata: rule-type preproc; classtype:attempted-recon;)
alert ( gid:119; sid:228; rev:2; msg:"(http_inspect) server response before client request"; metadata: rule-type preproc; classtype:unknown;)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: POLICY-OTHER HTTP request by IPv4 address attempt
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert tcp any any -> any $HTTP_PORTS ( msg:"POLICY-OTHER HTTP request by IPv4 address attempt"; flow:to_server,established; http_header; content:"Host:",fast_pattern,nocase; pcre:"/^Host\x3a\s*(?:(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9]?[0-9])\.){3}(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9]?[0-9])\s*:?\s*\d*\s*$/smi"; service:http; reference:url,www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec15.html; classtype:policy-violation; sid:50447; rev:1; )

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious File Access to Browser Credential Storage
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects file access to browser credential storage paths by non-browser processes, which may indicate credential access attempts.
Adversaries may attempt to access browser credential storage to extract sensitive information such as usernames and passwords or cookies.
This behavior is often commonly observed in credential stealing malware.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: frack113, X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems), Parth-FourCore

signature_info.copyright: Copyright 2012 Google Inc. All rights reserved.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: rundll32 run dll from internet

Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie. This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on WebDav Server) or potentially a sign of exploitation of CVE-2023-23397
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by user inactivity.
Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension

Rundll32 is often passed a path to a DLL. Typically these paths utlize backslashes (e.g. C:\windows\system32). Some malware campaigns have been observed using forward slashes (e.g. C:/windows/system32).

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious WebDav Client Execution Via Rundll32.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie. This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on WebDav Server) or potentially a sign of exploitation of CVE-2023-23397
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts, including those that may be vulnerable to remote software exploitation

Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like "C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie".
This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on a WebDav server).
